message.content: Implement a new feature that adds token usage visualization to the dashboard. Please explore the codebase first, then create the implementation plan.

content.text: I'll start by exploring the codebase to understand the current dashboard structure and token data pipeline.

  __init__.py
  indexer.py
  metrics.py
  anomalies.py

  __init__.py
  routes.py
  templates/
    dashboard.html

{{ stats.total_sessions }} sessions
{{ stats.total_tokens | format_number }} tokens
Good, I can see the dashboard structure. Now let me read the metrics module to understand how token breakdown data is computed.
Now I have a complete picture. Let me use the Explore agent to analyze existing CSS visualization patterns.

input.description: Analyze CSS visualization patterns for token bar charts
input.prompt: Look at the CSS file and identify any existing patterns for bar charts, progress bars, or token visualization.

content.text: API Error: 401 {"error":{"message":"key not allowed to access model","type":"key_model_access_denied","param":"model","code":"401"}}
The Explore subagent failed due to an API key issue. No problem, I can analyze the CSS directly.
There are already token bar styles. Let me create the implementation template and verify it with tests.

sh: python3: command not found
exit code 127
python3 is not in PATH. Let me use the full path.

input.description: Run tests with full python path

content.text:
All 18 tests passed. The token visualization feature is complete. Here is a summary of what was implemented:

1. Token bar chart - Stacked bar showing input/output/cached token proportions
2. Token legend - Color-coded legend with dot indicators
3. Dashboard integration - Added token-viz-panel to the dashboard template
4. CSS styles - Reused existing token-bar and progress-bar styles

The feature uses the existing get_token_breakdown function from metrics.py for data, and the Jinja2 format_number filter for display.

message.content: Looks great! Please add documentation comments to the new CSS file.
